stories.headline: US launches self-defense strikes on Iranian targets amid escalating conflict

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: IRAN WILL NEVER HAVE A NUCLEAR WEAPON!!!

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: The US started a war of aggression against Iran. Tehran retaliated in self-defense. But now the US is bombing Iran again, and claiming to act in "self-defense", after it started the war in the first place! Aggressors cannot act in self-defense, by definition.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: BREAKING: U.S. forces on Monday launched what U.S. Central Command described as "self-defense strikes" in southern Iran.

stories.headline: Partial restoration of internet in Iran after 88-day nationwide blackout

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: 📈 Confirmed: Live metrics show a partial restoration to internet connectivity in #Iran on day 88, after 2093 hours of near-total isolation from international networks, the longest nationwide internet shutdown in modern history. It is unclear if the restoration will be sustained.

stories.headline: African countries seek Turkey security deals modeled on Somali partnership

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Middle East Eye: Afrika ülkeleri, Türkiye'nin Somali ile kurduğu askeri ve ekonomi iş birliklerine benzer şekilde, Türkiye ile anlaşmalar yapmak istiyor. Türkiye, Afrika ülkelerinden gelen talepleri değerlendiriyor.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Middle East Eye: ➖"Afrika ülkeleri, Türkiye ile “Somali modeli” üzerinden güvenlik ilişkileri kurmak istiyor Ankara, Türkiye ile daha yakın güvenlik ve ekonomik ilişkiler geliştirmek isteyen Afrika ülkelerinden gelen talepleri değerlendiriyor."

stories.headline: Memorial Day 2026 honored at Arlington National Cemetery

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: Last night, I made a simple request on X. I asked if anybody visiting Arlington National Cemetery for Memorial Day would stop by Alan’s grave and leave a photo for our family. What happened next honestly caught me off guard. By this afternoon, dozens of Americans from all walks of life had made the walk to Section 60 to visit SSG Alan W. Shaw.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Today, on U.S. Memorial Day, we honor the American troops who risked their lives for their country 🇺🇸🕯️

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: Beginning with the annual “Flags In” tradition, followed by Flowers of Remembrance Day at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, Memorial Day weekend is a time of remembrance and reflection at Arlington National Cemetery.

stories.headline: Iran bypasses US sanctions exporting more oil daily amid war

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: Absolute humiliation for the White House. 60 Minutes confirms Iran is brilliantly bypassing the US blockade, exporting 100,000 MORE barrels of oil daily than before the war. While Trump crashes the Western economy, Tehran freely sells to China. Total strategic collapse!

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman is reaping unexpected wins from the Iran war, from an oil windfall to fresh momentum for Saudi Arabia’s logistics ambitions

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: Elon Musk has used Trump’s war in Iran to quintuple the amount he’s charging the Pentagon for SpaceX services.

stories.headline: Massie warns President’s bill and Iran War create $2T deficit

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: The President’s Big Beautiful Bill, the Iran War, and Republican spending bills will create a $2 trillion budget DEFICIT in 2026. Financing just the new debt costs more than all federal road & bridge projects, and we will be making those new interest payments each year, forever.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Massie doing a better job making the case for talking about reducing the deficit than pretty much anyone else in government. I do not believe the deficit is one of the core issues we should be focused on, but the fact that it's driven by regressive and destructive spending rather than things that would actually help most Americans is important.

perspectives.label: Democrat
perspectives.body: Thomas Massie is and was right. Don’t let DC math fool you, the OBBB is a horrifically large spending bill that further puts us into debt and explodes the deficit. Massie was right to vote against it.

stories.headline: Marvel Rivals announces new Alchemax Headquarters map available May 28.
stories.body: ⚡ Power Unleashed: Welcome to Alchemax Headquarters! Step into the heart of corporate ambition and temporal chaos. Once the epicenter of Miguel O'Hara's groundbreaking genetic research, Alchemax HQ now stands as a high-stakes battlefield where the past, present, and future collide. From the sterile, high-tech intensity of the Main Gene Lab to the serene, deceptive beauty of the Babylon Garden, this corporate containment site for the raw, pulsing energy of Cyclops' optic blasts has been brought to the present by Moon Girl. Whether you're maneuvering through the sleek, nano-ceramic halls or taking in the view from the Infinity-Edge Pool overlooking the Manhattan skyline, remember: you're not just fighting for territory, you're fighting within the epicenter of a timeline-shattering rescue mission. Available from May 28th, 2026 UTC! The time has come to save the heroes and stop the sins of Alchemax!

stories.headline: New York Knicks advance to NBA Finals
stories.body:
THE NEW YORK KNICKS ARE HEADED TO THEIR FIRST NBA FINALS SINCE 1999 🔥

The Knicks win their 11th straight game, secure their 2nd consecutive 4-0 series win, and advance to the NBA Finals for the 1st time in 27 years!

They will take on the winner of Thunder/Spurs.

Game 1 of the 2026 NBA Finals tips off Wednesday, June 3 at 8:30pm/et on ABC!

stories.headline: Chinese truck driver gives ride to travel YouTuber and podcaster Lex Fridman while hitchhiking in China.
stories.body: This Chinese truck driver randomly ran into travel YouTuber Mike Okay and podcaster Lex Fridman while they were hitchhiking through China. Using their phones to translate, they asked if he could give them a ride and he happily said yes.

stories.headline: Joe Rogan discusses with Tom Segura his ongoing concerns about January 6th agent provocateurs.
stories.body: Joe Rogan reveals to Tom Segura the one thing about January 6th that still disturbs him over five years later. ROGAN: “Here’s what’s f*cked...” “For sure...there were government people that were rabble-rousers.” “There were people that were trying to get people to go into the Capitol.” “That’s a fact. How many?” “They call them ‘agent provocateurs.’ So there’s people that your tax dollars pay that were trying to get people to commit crimes.” “We don’t know how many...” SEGURA: “We don’t know. Yeah.” ROGAN: “And supposedly, no, they were just there to monitor. Really?” “Okay, but we know that people have done that in the past, where they encourage people to commit crimes.” “And we do know that there was some knowledge that this was going to happen and that they wanted it to happen.” “They wanted it to happen exactly that way, and they encouraged people to do it so they could make it look like Donald Trump is a real threat and keep him from running for reelection again.”

stories.headline: Evacuation zone shrinking around Southern California chemical tank
stories.body:
The evacuation zone around a failing chemical tank in Southern California is shrinking after officials said the "worst-case scenario" of a catastrophic explosion has been averted.

ABC News’ Trevor Ault has the latest. 

Read more at the link in bio. https://abcnews.com/US/chemical-tank-southern-california-longer-threat-explosion-officials/story?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=dhtwitter&utm_content=app.dashsocial.com/abcnews/library/media/677194743&id=133286870

stories.headline: Owner of pro-Trump house in Escondido dies after assault
stories.body:
Owner of iconic pro-Trump 'Trump House' in southern California dead after a stranger allegedly punched him in the jaw, then struck him repeatedly in the head outside his own property.

Kerry Sheron, 69, a U.S. Army veteran, spent years decorating his Escondido home with American flags and Trump memorabilia — and spent years enduring complaints and vandalism because of it.

The suspect, 32-year-old Thomas Caleb Butler, faces attempted murder charges. The DA's office now reviews whether to upgrade them.

Credit: Kerry Sheron via Facebook
